briefdescription.para: This function gets the status on error bits.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on

simplesect.para: The error bits in the status register. Zero indicates no errors.

detaileddescription.para: Current transfer will finish gracefully. However, all queued SG transfers that have not started will be flushed from the hardware.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on

briefdescription.para: This function checks whether the hardware reset is done.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on

listitem.para: 1 if the reset has finished successfully
0 if the reset is not done

detaileddescription.para: It should be called before any other function calls to the driver.
It sets up the driver according to the hardware build. It resets the hardware at the end.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance that is working on
is the pointer to the hardware configuration structure
is the virtual address of the hardware instance. If address translation is not in use, please use the physical address

listitem.para: XST_INVALID_PARAM if word length is less than 4

briefdescription.para: This function checks whether the hardware is doing transfer.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on

listitem.para: 1 if the hardware is doing a transfer

briefdescription.para: This function configures KeyHole Write/Read Feature.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is WRITE/READ Select is the option to enable (TRUE) or disable (FALSE).

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS for success
XST_DEVICE_BUSY when transfer is in progress
XST_NO_FEATURE when not configured with feature

memberdef.argsstring: (XAxiCdma *InstancePtr, UINTPTR SrcAddr, UINTPTR DstAddr, int Length, XAxiCdma_CallBackFn SimpleCallBack, void *CallBackRef)
memberdef.name: XAxiCdma_SimpleTransfer

briefdescription.para: This function does one simple transfer submission.

detaileddescription.para:
It checks in the following sequence:
if engine is busy, cannot submit
if software is still handling the completion of the previous simple transfer, cannot submit
if engine is in SG mode and cannot switch to simple mode, cannot submit

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the driver instance
is the address of the source buffer
is the address of the destination buffer
is the length of the transfer
is the callback function for the simple transfer
is the callback reference pointer

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE for submission failure, maybe caused by: Another simple transfer is still going . Another SG transfer is still going
XST_INVALID_PARAM if: Length out of valid range [1: XAXICDMA_MAX_TRANSFER_LEN] Or, address not aligned when DRE is not built in

simplesect.para: Only set the callback function if using interrupt to signal the completion.If used in polling mode, please set the callback function to be NULL.

briefdescription.para: This function tries to set the interrupt coalescing threshold counter and the delay counter.

detaileddescription.para: If to set only one of the counters, set the value of the other counter to be XAXICDMA_COALESCE_NO_CHANGE.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the coalescing threshold to set to, the valid range is 1 to XAXICDMA_COALESCE_MAX.
is the delay timeout counter to set to, the valid range is 0 to XAXICDMA_DELAY_MAX. Setting a value of 0 disables the delay interrupt.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if hardware is in invalid state, for example, reset failed
XST_INVALID_PARAM if one of the counters is not in the valid range

briefdescription.para: This function gets the current setting of the interrupt coalescing threshold counter and the delay counter.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the return value for the coalescing counter setting
is the return value for the delay counter setting

simplesect.para: A zero coalescing threshold indicates invalid results

briefdescription.para: This function dumps the registers of this DMA instance.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
